product_overview: Loop Project Studio is a local project management and AI-assisted development workspace for registering existing codebases, bootstrapping new projects, and driving tasks through a guided 6-stage loop. It also includes an AI developer team manager for configuring agents and connecting chat workflows to either direct LLM interaction or a local IDE agent bridge.
core_goals: Register and organize local projects for ongoing work
Bootstrap new projects from a selectable template
Create and manage tasks tied to specific target files
Move tasks through a guided Plan, Build, Verify, Automate, Observe, and Learn workflow
Chat with AI developer agents to support implementation work
Run automated checks and manage git commit and publish actions from the workspace
Configure and maintain a roster of AI agents for development collaboration

features.name: View, search, and filter registered projects
features.description: Browse the project dashboard, narrow the list by project details or template, and open an existing project workspace.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Load the project list -> Type in the search box to filter projects by name or path -> Select a framework filter -> Open a project workspace
Navigate to / -> Load the project list -> Select a framework filter -> Clear the search criteria -> See the list update

features.name: Register an existing project
features.description: Add an already-existing local project to the registry by providing its name and absolute directory path, with optional preview URL and template metadata.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Open the register-existing flow -> Enter a project name -> Enter an absolute directory path -> Optionally browse directories and choose a folder -> Optionally add a preview URL and template -> Submit the form -> See the project appear in the list

features.name: Bootstrap a brand-new project
features.description: Create a new local project from a template and watch the scaffold process complete with live log output.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Open the bootstrap flow -> Enter a project name -> Enter an absolute directory path -> Choose a template -> Submit the form -> Watch the scaffold logs stream live -> Close the console -> See the new project in the list

features.name: Unregister a project
features.description: Remove a project from the registry while leaving the files on disk intact.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> View the project list -> Remove a project from the registry -> Confirm the browser prompt -> See the project disappear from the list

features.name: View project overview and task list
features.description: Inspect a project workspace, review git metadata, switch between Studio and Tasks views, and open an individual task workspace.
user_flows: Navigate to /[projectId] -> Load the project overview -> Review the project header and git information -> Switch to the Tasks view -> Search tasks or filter by status -> Open a task workspace
Navigate to /[projectId] -> Switch between Studio and Tasks tabs -> Review the task list and project context -> Open Settings to access agent configuration

features.name: Create a new task
features.description: Create a loop task for one or more target files and review the live risk-tier preview before submitting.
user_flows: Navigate to /[projectId] -> Open the create-task flow -> Enter a task title -> Add one or more target files -> Review the risk tier and safety-net checklist preview -> Submit the form -> See the new task in the task list

Navigate to /[projectId] -> Open the create-task flow -> Enter a task title -> Add a target file using autocomplete suggestions -> Review the risk tier preview update -> Submit the form -> See the task created

features.name: Walk through the 6-stage loop from the project page
features.description: Advance a selected task through the six-stage workflow without leaving the project overview page.
user_flows: Navigate to /[projectId] -> Choose a task in Studio -> Review the current stage workspace -> Advance through the stages one by one -> Reach the final stage summary
Navigate to /[projectId] -> Choose a task in Studio -> Open the full workspace for deeper task execution

features.name: Chat with the AI developer agent or bridge to an IDE agent
features.description: Send messages and attachments to the AI developer agent, switch between direct and bridged modes, and optionally delegate a multi-turn collaboration.
user_flows: Navigate to /[projectId]/tasks/[taskId] -> Type a chat message -> Send the message -> View the assistant response and live logs
Navigate to /[projectId]/tasks/[taskId] -> Attach a file or image -> Send the message -> View the response with the attached context
Navigate to /[projectId]/tasks/[taskId] -> Toggle IDE Agent Bridge mode -> See the waiting banner -> Cancel the bridge request
Navigate to /[projectId]/tasks/[taskId] -> Delegate to the AI Agent Team -> Let the background collaboration begin -> Monitor the conversation stream

features.name: Run the automated Verify+Automate pipeline
features.description: Execute automated unit tests, linting, and build checks with visible pass/fail progress.
user_flows: Navigate to /[projectId]/tasks/[taskId] -> Run checks -> Watch unit tests, lint, and build progress update -> See the final success or failure result

features.name: Stream execution logs
features.description: Watch live logs for chat, pipeline, and stage actions and copy the log buffer when needed.
user_flows: Navigate to /[projectId]/tasks/[taskId] -> Watch the log terminal update as actions run -> Copy the logs to the clipboard

features.name: Step through the 6-stage manual loop
features.description: Manually complete the Plan, Build, Verify, Automate, Observe, and Learn stages for a task, including review, execution, git actions, and retrospective submission.
user_flows: Navigate to /[projectId]/tasks/[taskId] -> Review the target file, risk tier, and safety-net checklist in Plan -> Approve the plan and advance to Build -> Copy the AI prompter template -> Mark code written and advance to Verify -> Choose a verification tool -> Execute verification -> Advance to Automate -> Run lint or typecheck and build checks -> Mark CI tests passed and advance to Observe -> Review git branch, commit, and diff -> Enter a commit message -> Commit changes and/or publish them -> Advance to Learn -> Fill in the retrospective answers -> Submit the learn stage
Navigate to /[projectId]/tasks/[taskId] -> Complete the stage-specific actions in order -> Observe each stage update as the workflow progresses

features.name: Live preview, commit, and publish from the Studio title bar
features.description: Preview the running project in an iframe and create or publish git commits from the task workspace title bar.
user_flows: Navigate to /[projectId]/tasks/[taskId] -> Switch to the Preview tab -> Type or reload the preview URL -> View the app inside the iframe
Navigate to /[projectId]/tasks/[taskId] -> Open the commit and publish flow -> Enter a commit message -> Publish the changes -> See the commit action complete

features.name: View commit history and revert
features.description: Inspect recent commits and revert the workspace back to an earlier commit.
user_flows: Navigate to /[projectId]/tasks/[taskId] -> View the commit history -> Review recent commit changesets -> Revert to a prior commit -> Confirm the revert prompt

features.name: Configure the AI provider API key
features.description: Save an AI provider API key locally in the browser for use in chat workflows.
user_flows: Navigate to /agents -> Enter an API key -> See the local save confirmation

features.name: List, add, edit, and delete AI agents
features.description: Manage a local roster of AI developer agents by defining their role, model, skills, and system instructions.
user_flows: Navigate to /agents -> Open the add-agent flow -> Enter the agent name, role, model engine, skills, and system instructions -> Optionally generate a prompt from the role and skills -> Submit the form -> See the new agent in the roster
Navigate to /agents -> Edit an existing agent's name, role, model, skills, or system prompt -> Save the configuration -> See the updated agent details
Navigate to /agents -> Remove an agent -> Confirm the deletion prompt -> See the agent removed from the roster

routes.description: Project list / dashboard - register, bootstrap, and manage local projects
Project workspace - overview, task list, and walkthrough of the 6-stage loop
Task workspace ("Studio window") - chat with AI agent, run pipeline, step through Plan/Build/Verify/Automate/Observe/Learn stages, git commit/push
AI Developer Team page - configure API key and manage AI agents

features.name: View, search, and filter registered projects
features.description: Load registered projects and filter them client-side by name/path and framework template
user_interactions: Load the page and see the list of registered projects
Type in the search box to filter by name/path
Select a framework in the filter dropdown
Click "Open Studio" on a project card to navigate to /[projectId]

features.name: Register an existing project
features.description: Register a project that already exists on disk by pointing to its absolute directory path
user_interactions: Click "Register Existing" to open the register modal
Fill in Name and Absolute Directory Path
Optionally click "Browse" to open FolderPicker and navigate server directories
Optionally fill Preview URL and select a Template
Submit the form
See the modal close and the project list refresh

features.name: Bootstrap (scaffold) a brand-new project
features.description: Create a new project from a template and stream the scaffold log output live
user_interactions: Click "Bootstrap Project" to open the bootstrap modal
Fill in Name, Absolute Directory Path, and choose a Template (Next.js App / Vite React / Empty Directory)
Submit the form
Watch the live scaffold log stream until completion
Click "Close Console" and see the project list refresh

features.name: Unregister a project
features.description: Remove a project from the registry without deleting files on disk
user_interactions: Click the trash icon on a project card
Confirm the browser confirmation dialog
See the project removed from the list

features.name: View project overview and task list
features.description: See project header info (branch/commit) and browse tasks via Studio or Tasks tab
user_interactions: Load the project page and see project header and git info
Switch between "Studio" and "Tasks" tabs
In the Tasks tab, type in the search box and pick a Status filter
Click "Enter Loop" on a task row to navigate to /[projectId]/tasks/[taskId]
Click "Settings" to navigate to /agents

features.name: Create a new task
features.description: Initialize a new loop task against one or more target files, with a live risk-tier preview
user_interactions: Click "Create Task" (toolbar button or empty-state CTA) to open the create-task modal
Type a Task Title
Add one or more Target Files via the tag input, using autocomplete suggestions
See the Risk Tier and safety-net checklist preview update after selecting a target file
Submit the form
See the modal close and the new task appear in the list

features.name: Walkthrough a task through the 6-stage loop from the project page
features.description: Drive Plan/Build/Verify/Automate/Observe/Learn stages for a selected task without leaving the project page
user_interactions: In the Studio tab, pick a task chip to drive
Step through the stage workspace and advance stages
Click "Full workspace" to jump to /[projectId]/tasks/[taskId]

features.name: Chat with the AI developer agent or bridge to an IDE agent
features.description: Send chat messages (with optional file/image attachments) either directly to an LLM or bridged to a local IDE coding agent
user_interactions: Type a message in the chat textarea and press Enter to send (Shift+Enter for newline)
Attach a file/image via the "+" menu, or paste an image directly into the textarea
Toggle the "IDE Agent Bridge" icon to switch between direct chat and bridged mode
When bridged, see a "waiting for IDE agent" banner and optionally click Cancel to abort
Click "Delegate to AI Agent Team" to start a background multi-turn collaboration

features.name: Run the automated Verify+Automate pipeline
features.description: Run unit tests, lint, and build server-side with a live pass/fail checklist
user_interactions: Click "Run checks"
Watch the per-step checklist (Unit tests / Lint / Build) update with pass/fail/exit-code
See a success or failure banner when the run completes

features.name: Stream execution logs
features.description: View a live-streaming log terminal for chat, pipeline, and stage actions
user_interactions: Watch the log terminal auto-update as actions run
Click "Copy Logs" to copy the buffer to the clipboard

features.name: Step through the 6-stage manual loop
features.description: Manually advance a task through Plan, Build, Verify, Automate, Observe, and Learn stages, each with its own actions
user_interactions: Plan: review target file, Risk Tier, and safety-net checklist, then click Approve Plan & Advance to Build
Build: copy the AI prompter template, then click Code Written & Advance to Verify
Verify: choose Vitest or Playwright, click Execute, then Advance to Automate
Automate: click Run Lint Check or Run Typecheck & Build, then CI Tests Passed & Advance to Observe
Observe: view git branch/commit and diff, enter a commit message, click Commit All and/or Publish, then Advance to Learn
Learn: fill in 3 retrospective text answers and submit

features.name: Live preview, commit, and publish from the Studio title bar
features.description: Preview the running project in an iframe and commit/push changes from the title bar
user_interactions: Click "Commit & Publish" to open the commit popover
Type a commit message and click Publish (commits, then pushes if commit succeeds)
Switch between Preview/Code/Diff tabs (only Preview is functional; Code and Diff are placeholders)
Type/reload/open-in-new-tab the preview iframe URL

features.name: View commit history and revert
features.description: List recent git commits and revert to a prior commit
user_interactions: View the list of recent commits with insertions/deletions
Click "Revert" on a commit and confirm the dialog

features.name: Configure the AI provider API key
features.description: Save an Anthropic or Google AI Studio API key to browser local storage for use in chat
user_interactions: Type an API key into the password-style input
See a transient "API Key saved locally." confirmation

features.name: List, add, edit, and delete AI agents
features.description: Manage a roster of AI developer agents with role, model engine, skills, and system prompt
user_interactions: Click "Add Agent" and fill Name, Role, Model Engine, Expertise Skills, and System Instructions
Optionally click "Generate from role & skills" to auto-fill the system prompt
Submit to create the agent
Edit Name/Role/Model/Skills/System Prompt inline on an existing agent card and click "Save Config"
Click the trash icon on an agent card and confirm to delete it

known_limitations.issue: POST /api/loop-projects/[projectId]/tasks/reorder exists in the API but is not called by any UI component - no drag-and-drop reordering exists in the frontend
known_limitations.impact: This endpoint is unreachable via the UI and should not be treated as a testable frontend feature

known_limitations.issue: PreviewPane defaults the preview iframe to /apps when a project has no previewUrl, but /apps does not exist in this codebase (leftover from a removed old app)
known_limitations.impact: Projects registered without an explicit Preview URL will show a 404/blank iframe by default

known_limitations.issue: PreviewPane's Code and Diff tabs are non-functional placeholders
known_limitations.impact: No real content renders in those tabs; do not assert real diff/code content there

known_limitations.issue: Toggling a skill checkbox on an agent card updates local state only; it is not persisted until Save Config is clicked separately
known_limitations.impact: A test that checks a skill box and expects it saved without clicking Save Config will fail

known_limitations.issue: getPipelineStatus() derives Verify/Build pass/fail badges by string-matching the latest activity message text rather than a structured status field
known_limitations.impact: Fragile heuristic; unexpected activity message wording could show incorrect check status

known_limitations.issue: The IDE Agent Bridge flow depends on an external IDE agent writing to .antigravity/bridge.json
known_limitations.impact: Cannot be fully exercised end-to-end by an automated frontend test without mocking; only UI state (pending banner, cancel) is testable

known_limitations.issue: README.md describes a different, older product (Adapter Library with Zero Trust login, /apps, /login, /callback) that no longer exists in this codebase
known_limitations.impact: Stale documentation; do not rely on README.md for route/feature ground truth
